candidate.headline: Italy brings IRIDE Earth observation service online
candidate.summary: Italy's IRIDE Earth observation marketplace went live July 1 for government users only (civil protection, security/defense, public administration); ASI says commercial registration will be enabled in the future. Only the HEO multispectral constellation is operational so far (15 satellites across 3 launches); the eventual EUR1.1 billion, 68-satellite, six-constellation buildout is being progressively transferred from ESA to ASI.

held.reason: Scope question, not a sourcing problem: IRIDE is ASI's own government-owned EO constellation (ESA implementing agency transitioning ownership to ASI), not a commercial operator selling to a government customer -- contrast the Portugal/Norway ICEYE sovereign-contract items already published, which are agile commercial operators winning government business. The marketplace is government-only today ('commercial registration will be enabled in the future'), which reads closer to the excluded Aeolus-2 institutional-program precedent (SWEEP_MEMORY 2026-07-05-Q) than to an in-scope commercial EO event, but the EUR1.1B/68-satellite scale and stated future commercial angle make this a real judgment call rather than a clean exclusion. Flagging for Florian rather than deciding unilaterally.
